name: UR-ENERGY INC
business: Ur-Energy Inc. is a uranium mining company that produces uranium oxide (U3O8) through in situ recovery and sells it to nuclear utilities under multi-year sales agreements. The company operates the Lost Creek ISR facility in Wyoming and is developing the Shirley Basin project.

exposures.sentence: Uranium fuels carbon-free, emission-free nuclear power which is a clean, cost-effective, and reliable form of electrical power.

Efforts to increase the availability of nuclear power to help satisfy the increasing demand for electricity have been driven in part by the emergence of artificial intelligence ( AI ) and the expansion of the data center industry.

Efforts to increase the availability of nuclear power to help satisfy the increasing demand for electricity have been driven in part by the emergence of artificial intelligence ( AI ) and the expansion of the data center industry.

catalysts.event: Commence production and commission Shirley Basin operations
catalysts.sentence: Construction and development at the site, including construction of the satellite plant, is well advanced and we plan to commence production and commission Shirley Basin operations in 2026.

catalysts.event: Bring several header houses online in MU1 Phase 2 at Lost Creek
catalysts.sentence: During 2026 H1, we anticipate bringing several header houses online in MU1 Phase 2 as we continue to progress toward full plant capacity production.

catalysts.event: Construct water treatment facility at Lost Creek
catalysts.sentence: engineering work has progressed for a filtration and wastewater treatment facility, which we plan to construct in 2026 after detailed design work is complete.

catalysts.event: Commence aquifer testing at Lost Soldier project
catalysts.sentence: We will commence aquifer testing in 2026 Q1 and plan to initiate baseline environmental studies in 2026 in anticipation of possible permitting to advance the project.

catalysts.event: First header house in MU1 Phase 2 brought online
catalysts.sentence: The first of those header houses was brought online in February 2026.

catalysts.event: Pre-operational inspection by Wyoming Uranium Recovery Program at Shirley Basin
catalysts.sentence: The URP began its pre-operational inspection in late February 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global nuclear capacity could more than double by 2050 per IAEA
tamClaims.sentence: The most recent projections of the International Atomic Energy Agency are that global nuclear capacity could more than double by 2050, and the World Nuclear Association ( WNA ) has called for nuclear power generation to triple by 2050.

tamClaims.claim: Global uranium requirements could increase to about 86,000 tonnes by 2030 and 150,000 tonnes by 2040 per WNA
tamClaims.sentence: In its September 2025 report, the WNA projected that global uranium requirements could increase by approximately one-third to about 86,000 metric tonnes by 2030 and to approximately 150,000 metric tonnes by 2040.

tamClaims.claim: Data center share of U.S. electricity consumption to grow from 4.4% in 2023 to 7-12% by 2028 per DOE
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. Department of Energy ( DOE ) has reported that the data center industry consumed approximately 4.4% of U.S. electricity in 2023, and projects that its share of consumption will grow to 7 to 12% by 2028.
